questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: The principal ore mineral of lead is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Galena (PbS) is the chief lead ore; chalcopyrite=Cu, sphalerite=Zn, cassiterite=Sn.

**Working.**
Galena.

**Answer.** Galena

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: Chromite and platinum-group deposits typically form by
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Chromite/PGE concentrate by magmatic (cumulate) segregation in mafic–ultramafic layered intrusions.

**Working.**
Magmatic segregation.

**Answer.** Magmatic segregation

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: Bauxite, the ore of aluminium, forms primarily by
options: intense lateritic weathering in the tropics
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bauxite is a residual lateritic weathering product (tropical/humid).

**Working.**
Lateritic weathering.

**Answer.** Lateritic weathering

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: Which deposit types are typically epigenetic/hydrothermal?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Porphyry Cu, MVT Pb–Zn and vein gold are hydrothermal; BIF is a chemical sedimentary (syngenetic) deposit.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 4.

**Answer.** Hydrothermal deposits

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: The Kolar belt of Karnataka is famous historically for
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Kolar Gold Fields — Archean greenstone-hosted lode gold.

**Working.**
Gold.

**Answer.** Gold mining
